content:
Hi all!

I just sent my first patch series to the list:

[PATCH v1 0/3] rust: alloc: add Vec shrinking methods
https://lore.kernel.org/lkml/20260130205424.261700-1-shivamklr@cock.li/

I ran into some issues and have a few etiquette questions:

Context: Due to an email quota issue, patches 2/3 and 3/3 were sent ~12 hours after the cover letter and patch 1/3.

Questions:

  1. Thread breakage - The thread view on lore seems broken (patches 2-3 aren't linked properly). Is this because of the time gap, or did I miss something in the headers?

  2. Base commit location - I included base-commit: only in the cover letter. Should it be in every patch?

  3. Branch targeting - This series depends on a commit pending on char-misc-next, but I didn't use [PATCH char-misc-next] syntax. Should I have?

  4. Time gap tolerance - How long is too long between parts of the same series? Should I have re-sent as v2?

  5. Recovery options - If the threading is too broken, what's the proper fix? Resend as v2 with correct In-Reply-To headers?

1. It's because they are missing the In-Reply-To: header. Each patch should reference the message-id of the cover letter here.
  2. Just in cover letter is fine. But in your case the commit hash is meaningless because it's the commit hash of your local copy of the pending commit (I'm guessing).
  3. Just [PATCH] is fine, but you should say in the cover letter what you depend on. E.g., with a lore link to the patch it depends on.
  4. One should generally wait at least a few days between new versions, but the "this series was sent incorrectly" case is an exception. There it's ok to immediately send a v2 (or just resend v1), and then leave a reply on the broken version that you resent it.
  5. See above.

For 1, in lore.kernel.org, if you click on "permalink" on a given message, and then scroll down, you will see instructions that Lore generates for git send-email on how you should set up a reply to any given message.
If you want, you can send the patches that are missing with that git send-email command manually. But sending a new version of everything is fine too.
An alternative approach for 1: Download mbox version of the message (you can search for "download: mbox" on the page) and import it on the email client (e.g., claws-mail) and reply from there.

Thank you everyone.
I understand now - the In-Reply-To: header is key for threading. Each patch should reference the cover letter's Message-ID so lore can link them properly.

  1. Just in cover letter is fine. But in your case the commit hash is meaningless because it's the commit hash of your local copy of the pending commit (I'm guessing).

Alice Ryhl you're right, I noticed it's in my local patch file but gets stripped from the email. Makes sense that it's just local metadata. I will include a lore link to dependencies in the cover letter instead.

An alternative approach for 1: Download mbox version of the message (you can search for "download: mbox" on the page) and import it on the email client (e.g., claws-mail) and reply from there.

@Onur Özkan I will take a look at it.

I know what the problem is now, I tried v2 but ran out of the email send quota link
I guess I will have to switch to another email provider that has better daily quotas.

You can use the b4 sending web endpoint:
https://b4.docs.kernel.org/en/latest/contributor/send.html#configuring-the-web-endpoint

Alice Ryhl said:

You can use the b4 sending web endpoint:
https://b4.docs.kernel.org/en/latest/contributor/send.html#configuring-the-web-endpoint

I will take a look. For now, I used the same In-Reply-To in the late submitted patch. Feedback is welcome on the patch series.
I'm off sick right now, but I will look later
Get well soon! No rush at all on the review, I will wait for your feedback whenever you are back and feeling better
shivamklr has marked this topic as resolved.
